rescue_reasoning: Keep because the policy statement is explicitly resource-efficiency/circular-economy oriented. Verbatim text: “lending libraries, repair clinics, and reuse exchanges … for … goods that reduce production and consumption of goods.” Reducing production/consumption via reuse/repair directly supports material/resource efficiency and aligns with the stated canonical mechanism “reuse_and_repair_programs → embodied_emissions_avoidance.” Climate screen is explicit_self, so it should remain in the climate dataset despite missing a specific outcome token in the gate.
policy_statement: By 2024, encourage/facilitate inclusive neighborhood-scale lending libraries, repair clinics, and reuse exchanges for tools/electronics/furniture and other goods (RPC.3-3).
mechanism_description: Encourage or facilitate lending libraries, repair clinics, and reuse exchanges so goods are reused and repaired instead of newly produced, reducing production- and consumption-related emissions.
primary_causal_pathway: Same service (utility from goods) → ↓ material throughput + ↓ new production → ↓ embodied energy/use-phase upstream emissions → ↓ GHG emissions
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Changed activity/system: Households and communities shift from buying new items to borrowing, sharing, repairing, refurbishing, and exchanging goods (tools, electronics, furniture, etc.), extending product lifetimes and increasing utilization rates.  
2) Mediating process: Reduced demand for virgin-material production lowers industrial activity across supply chains (extraction, processing, manufacturing, packaging, and logistics), thereby reducing lifecycle/embodied energy use and associated CO₂e.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Net decrease in GHG emissions primarily via avoided embodied emissions; secondary reductions may occur via decreased waste treatment emissions (landfill/incineration) and avoided emissions from producing replacement goods.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is avoiding embodied GHG emissions by reducing the need to manufacture new products through increased reuse, sharing, and repair.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Increasing reuse/repair/sharing reduces material throughput and the need for new product manufacturing, leading to avoided embodied emissions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: Although these programs operate in the waste sector and can reduce landfill/incineration, the mechanism as stated centers on substituting reuse/repair for new production—an efficiency/circularity pathway rather than end-of-pipe waste emissions control.  
3) Edge cases applied: If a specific policy instance focuses mainly on landfill diversion or methane capture, it could shift toward Mitigation via waste-treatment emissions; however, that is not this canonical mechanism, which targets avoided production.  
4) Applies to ALL instances sharing this mechanism: Any policy that encourages lending libraries, repair clinics, and reuse exchanges operates through extending lifetimes/increasing utilization and thereby avoiding embodied emissions, making Resource Efficiency the consistent primary category.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Any additional categorization (e.g., whether benefits are mainly from metals, electronics, furniture; or equity/community resilience co-benefits) requires policy-level evidence and is not assigned here.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Some instances may deliver comparable or larger benefits from avoided waste-treatment emissions (e.g., reduced incineration) or could increase travel/energy use for repair events; classification remains Resource Efficiency because the canonical dominant intent and typical effect is avoided new production (embodied emissions).
key_indicators: “By 2024, encourage or facilitate”; “lending libraries”; “repair clinics”; “reuse exchanges”; “reduce production and consumption of goods”
co_benefits: Waste reduction/diversion; affordability and cost savings for households; community resilience/social cohesion; local skills/jobs in repair; reduced landfill/incineration impacts
instance_edge_case_notes: “Encourage or facilitate” could be implemented via grants, space provision, partnerships, or outreach; the instrument is therefore best captured as an incentive/programmatic support category rather than a specific regulatory or procurement tool.
